purpose.summary: This COBOL program interacts with an IMS database to retrieve and update pending authorization details. It retrieves authorization summary and detail segments, and allows for marking authorizations as fraudulent or removing fraud tags. The program is likely part of a CICS application, using CICS SYNCPOINT for transaction management.
purpose.business_context: Fraud detection and authorization management within a financial system.

inputs.name: PAUTSUM0
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Summary segment, containing account ID and authorization key.

inputs.name: PAUTDTL1
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Details segment, containing authorization details and fraud indicators.

inputs.name: WS-ACCT-ID
inputs.description: Account ID passed to the program.

inputs.name: WS-AUTH-KEY
inputs.description: Authorization Key passed to the program.

outputs.name: PAUTDTL1
outputs.description: Updated Pending Authorization Details segment, reflecting fraud tagging or removal.

outputs.name: WS-MESSAGE
outputs.description: Message displayed on the CICS screen to the user, indicating success or failure of the operation.

called_programs.purpose: Displays the authorization view screen to the user, showing authorization details and any error messages.

business_rules.description: An authorization can be marked as fraudulent or have the fraud tag removed.
business_rules.logic_summary: The program updates the PAUTDTL1 segment in the IMS database based on user input.

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the structure of the PAUTSUM0 IMS segment.
Defines the structure of the PAUTDTL1 IMS segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph retrieves the pending authorization summary and details from the IMS database. It first calls SCHEDULE-PSB to schedule the PSB. It then moves the account ID and authorization key to the corresponding fields in the PAUTSUM0 segment. A GU (Get Unique) command is executed to retrieve the PAUTSUM0 segment based on the account ID. If the segment is found, a GNP (Get Next within Parent) command is executed to retrieve the PAUTDTL1 segment based on the authorization key. Error handling is performed by checking the DIBSTAT return code after each IMS call. If an error occurs, an error message is constructed and displayed using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN. The paragraph sets AUTHS-EOF or AUTHS-NOT-EOF flags based on the IMS return codes. It consumes WS-ACCT-ID and WS-AUTH-KEY as input and retrieves data from PAUTSUM0 and PAUTDTL1. It outputs data to PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY and PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS.

This paragraph retrieves the next pending authorization detail record from the IMS database. It executes a GNP (Get Next within Parent) command to retrieve the next PAUTDTL1 segment. Error handling is performed by checking the DIBSTAT return code after the IMS call. If an error occurs, an error message is constructed and displayed using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN. The paragraph sets AUTHS-EOF or AUTHS-NOT-EOF flags based on the IMS return codes. It consumes no explicit input parameters but relies on the current position within the IMS database. It outputs data to PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS. This paragraph is likely part of a loop to iterate through multiple authorization details for a given account.

This paragraph updates the pending authorization details in the IMS database. It moves the contents of WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD to the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS area. Then, it executes a REPL (Replace) command to update the PAUTDTL1 segment in the IMS database. After the update, it checks the DIBSTAT return code. If the update is successful, it performs TAKE-SYNCPOINT to commit the changes to the database and displays a success message using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN. If the update fails, it performs ROLL-BACK to undo any changes and displays an error message using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN. The paragraph consumes WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD as input and updates the PAUTDTL1 segment. The display statement on line 93 is likely for debugging purposes. The business logic involves setting or removing a fraud flag on the authorization record.

This paragraph issues a CICS SYNCPOINT command to commit the changes made to the IMS database. This ensures that all updates are permanently saved. It does not consume any specific inputs or produce any specific outputs other than the implicit commit of database changes. It is called after a successful update of the authorization details. If the SYNCPOINT fails, CICS will typically handle the error and potentially abend the transaction. This paragraph is crucial for maintaining data consistency and integrity in the CICS environment.

This paragraph issues a CICS SYNCPOINT ROLLBACK command to undo any changes made to the IMS database since the last syncpoint. This is used to handle errors during the update process and ensure data integrity. It does not consume any specific inputs or produce any specific outputs other than the implicit rollback of database changes. It is called when an error occurs during the update of authorization details. This paragraph is essential for maintaining data consistency in case of failures.

This paragraph schedules the PSB (Program Specification Block) for accessing the IMS database. It executes a DLI SCHD command with the PSB name. If the PSB has already been scheduled, it first terminates the existing schedule with a DLI TERM command and then schedules it again. This ensures that the program has access to the required IMS segments. Error handling is performed by checking the DIBSTAT return code after the SCHD command. If an error occurs, an error message is constructed and displayed using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN. The paragraph consumes the PSB-NAME as input and makes the IMS database accessible. The NODHABEND option prevents a U0999 abend if the PSB cannot be scheduled.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK, SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND, or END-OF-DB after reading Auth Summary
error_handling.action: Set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and display it using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK, SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND, or END-OF-DB after reading Auth Details
error_handling.action: Set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and display it using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK, SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND, or END-OF-DB after reading next Auth
error_handling.action: Set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and display it using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK after updating Auth Details
error_handling.action: Perform ROLL-BACK, set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and display it using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK after scheduling PSB
error_handling.action: Set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and display it using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.
